plugins.name: understanding-konflux-resources
plugins.description: Quick reference for Konflux CI/CD Custom Resources (RP, RPA, ITS) - helps users understand Applications, Components, Snapshots, IntegrationTestScenarios, ReleasePlans, namespace placement, common abbreviations, and confusions

plugins.name: investigating-failed-plrs
plugins.description: Investigate why a specific Konflux PipelineRun failed. Drills through PLR conditions, failed TaskRuns, pod status, container exit codes, and timing via KubeArchive.

plugins.name: investigating-slow-builds
plugins.description: Investigate why Konflux builds/pipeline runs are slow. Analyzes kueue queue time, task durations, quota pressure, and Prometheus metrics.

plugins.name: navigating-github-to-konflux-pipelines
plugins.description: Use when GitHub PR or branch has failing checks and you need to find Konflux pipeline information (cluster, namespace, PipelineRun name). Teaches gh CLI commands to identify Konflux checks (filter out Prow/SonarCloud), extract PipelineRun URLs from builds and integration tests, and parse URLs for kubectl debugging.

plugins.name: working-with-provenance
plugins.description: Use when tracing Konflux builds from image references, finding build logs from artifacts, or verifying source commits for container images - extracts provenance attestations to navigate from images back to builds and source code

plugins.name: component-build-status
plugins.description: Use to trigger component builds, releases or get a component or application status

plugins.name: konflux-architecture
plugins.description: Use when needing to understand Konflux architecture, design decisions, service responsibilities, APIs, build/test/release workflows, or how Konflux components fit together.

plugins.name: using-trusted-maven-dependencies
plugins.description: Use when updating a Maven project to source its dependencies from a trusted Maven repository instead of Maven Central.

plugins.name: red-hat-konflux-teams
plugins.description: Use when needing to know which Red Hat team owns a Konflux-related repository (konflux-ci, hermetoproject, conforma orgs), which JIRA project or component to file issues against, or how the Red Hat Konflux engineering organization maps to the codebase.
